text.title: go-project-structure Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for go-project-structure as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Defining the interface in the repository package and importing it in the service.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Inverts the dependency — service must import repository. Idiomatic Go reverses this.

failure-mode.description: var DB *sql.DB at package level, accessed directly from handlers.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Global state is untestable (cannot inject a mock), causes data races when reset between tests, and makes the dependency graph invisible.

failure-mode.description: Creating a pkg/ directory for internal helpers, business logic, or service code that no external module imports.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: pkg/ signals public API. Once there, consumers depend on it. Renaming to internal/ is a breaking change. Reject pkg/ without a named external consumer.

failure-mode.description: Package A imports B, package B imports A — produces compile error: import cycle not allowed.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cyclic imports reveal a design flaw: shared logic should move to a third package that neither A nor B owns.

failure-mode.description: LLMs reflexively create a pkg/ directory and put helpers there.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: This leaks internals once anyone imports the module. Reject pkg/ without a named external consumer.

failure-mode.description: Generated cmd/api/main.go often skips defer srv.Shutdown(ctx) or shuts down the DB pool before draining requests.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require an audit checklist. Graceful shutdown must close DB, drain queues, cancel contexts in the right order.

failure-mode.description: Agents drop graceful shutdown for worker binaries entirely.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require a select { case <-quit: case <-ctx.Done(): } pattern in every long-running goroutine.

failure-mode.description: LLMs use package-level var DB *sql.DB; this works for tutorials and breaks for tests.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Insist on constructor injection. Global state prevents parallel test execution and mocking.

failure-mode.description: Agents propose cyclic imports (handler → service → handler) when extracting interfaces.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require a dependency graph review before commit. Cyclic imports reveal design flaws.

failure-mode.description: Generated getEnvInt and similar helpers silently return defaults on parse error.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require an explicit error-on-malformed mode. A missing PORT should fail startup, not default silently.

failure-mode.description: An agent migrating from flat layout often rewrites import paths globally and breaks vendored modules.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require a go build ./... proof and a check for replace directives in go.mod.

failure-mode.description: Any move into internal/ from a previously exported path is a public-API break.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents underestimate this. Require a human checkpoint before breaking public APIs.

failure-mode.description: Agents emit Dockerfile without USER nobody and without distroless base.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Review for prod-readiness. No binaries should run as root.
